text: The campus in Sector H-12 Islamabad was initiated in 2008. The campus is spread over 800 acres. Includes all sports facilities. Cricket, hockey football  Besides NUST Headquarters, the schools and institutes that were relocated to this campus include School of Civil and Environmental Engineering, School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering, School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, School of Chemical and Materials Engineering, Research Center for Modeling and Simulation, School of Natural Sciences (formerly known as Center for Advanced Mathematics and Physics), Atta-ur-Rehman School of Applied Bio-sciences, School of Art Design and Architecture, NUST Business School, School of Social Sciences and Humanities, Professional Development Center, Technology Incubation Center, Marketing and Industrial Relations Organization, NUST Publishing and NUST Outreach and Talent Hunt Initiative. Well maintained by the authorities. Fully recommend for the students to get visionary education in the best environment.

The National Science and Technology Park (NSTP) in Islamabad, Pakistan, is a hub for innovation and research, fostering collaboration among technology-based organizations and commercial tenants. Situated in NUST University, the park aims to develop commercial applications for knowledge in association with its tenants. The park's focus on sustainable development and industrial clusters dedicated to specific national problems showcases its commitment to addressing critical challenges. With state-of-the-art facilities and a collaborative environment, the NSTP plays a vital role in driving technological advancement and economic growth in Pakistan.
